mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, causing equity investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of observations where UNRATE crosses above 4.5, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production signals recovering manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity buying pressure and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >70% of recovery-phase observations, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 248, inflationary pressures force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches 248, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in >50% of expansionary months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly retail sales indicate a synchronized demand contraction, which forces immediate equity repricing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The dual decline signal yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index surpasses 105, manufacturing output operates near capacity constraints, signaling strong economic momentum that supports corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 105 coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold predictive power vanishes after 2015.
